title: Muse Group says musicians have ‘clear boundaries’ on AI usage - Music Ally
text:
Muse Group is the parent group of Ultimate Guitar, Hal Leonard, MuseScore and Audacity, so it’s got a sizeable community of musicians – handy when it wants to find out what they think about new technologies like AI.
That’s what it has done with a survey of 1,200 US-based musicians who are active users of its products. The findings show some interesting nuances in how they feel about AI.
78% said that they are open to AI tools, and 70% are already using them in their music-making processes. Of the latter group, 54% use AI for noise removal and audio cleanup and 46% for ‘idea generation’.
38% are using AI tools for stem separation; 35% for pitch correction; 32% for practice support; and 28% for transcribing music to notes or MIDI.
What about full-blown music generation? Muse Group says that only 18% were open to that “and even then only with guidance and full editability”. So 82% are not open to it.
Other findings from the report include Gen-Z being “the most skeptical and polarised group” when it comes to AI views. One surprise is that only 41% of respondents “reject training on others’ work without permission” while just 40% reject “copying artist styles without consent”.

here

Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
